normalization.notes: Store Burmese Myanmar text in NFC while preserving source order for vowel signs, medials, asat, anusvara, tone marks, and stacked signs. Analysis tooling may inspect orthographic syllables and rendered clusters without rewriting source text.

segmentation.pedagogical_units: consonant bases, inherent vowel, dependent vowel signs, medials, asat, anusvara, tone marks, stacked forms, and common syllable frames
segmentation.notes: Burmese often omits spaces between words and relies on orthographic syllables. Count dictionary words, syllable frames, visible clusters, and teachable marks separately because Myanmar shaping can reorder or stack marks around the base.
